pr.title: ft: decompile Kirby & Ice Climbers special moves
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled 17 fighter special-move functions for Kirby copy abilities and Ice Climbers specials, replacing /// # stubs with 100% objdiff-matching C. The PR covered Kirby Ness, Sheik, Pikachu/Pichu, Bowser/Giga Bowser, and Link/Young Link neutral-B helpers plus Popo/Ice Climbers up-B and down-B helpers. It also added required callback/header prototypes and refined Popo special attributes. Verification reported main.dol OK and decomp.dev showed +2676 matched bytes, +0.07%, with 17 new 100% matches.

key_files.note: Decompiled Kirby-copy Ness neutral-B end/start/collision helpers and added ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr.
Decompiled Kirby-copy Pikachu/Pichu and Bowser/Giga Bowser ground-to-air collision transitions plus Link/Young Link special-N index helper.
Decompiled Kirby-copy Sheik neutral-B ground and air end animations.
Decompiled Ice Climbers up-B and down-B entry/physics/animation/state helpers.
Split padding into ftIceClimberAttributes x88, x8C, and x90 floats used by the new Popo air up-B code.
Added prototypes for callback targets fn_80100E0C and fn_80105978.
Added bool prototype for ftNn_Init_8012309C used by Popo throw animation logic.

what_changed: In ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, decompiled ftKb_NsSpecialNEnd_Anim, ftKb_NsSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101560, ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101618, and ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Coll.
Added static inline ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr to copy Ness-copy charge timing fields from ftKb_DatAttrs, clear fv.kb.ns_flash_gobj, and set charge_release_delay before end-animation handling.
Kirby Ness-copy air end now branches between ftCo_Fall_Enter and ftCo_80096900 based on specialn_ns_freefall_toggle; both ground and air end reset part rotation with ftPartSetRotX.

Kirby Pr neutral-B start helpers choose R/L motion IDs from facing_dir, reset cmd_vars, call ftAnim_8006EBA4 and ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010131C, and set vertical velocity/dat-attr fields for ground versus air.
ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Coll converts grounded collision to air motion with ftCommon_8007D5D4, preserves cur_anim_frame, uses flag 0x0C4C5092U, and installs death/take/deal/x21F8 callbacks.
In ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, decompiled ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialNStart_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialNEnd_Coll, and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394.
Kirby Pikachu/Pichu and Bowser/Giga Bowser collision helpers select motion IDs by fv.kb.hat.kind, preserving current animation frame and match-critical motion flags.
ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394 now returns a Link special-N index for active Kirby Link or Young Link copy states by subtracting the relevant base motion ID, otherwise ftLk_SpecialNIndex_None.
In ftKb_SpecialNZd.c, decompiled ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim; both increment mv.kb.specialhi.x0 and set x4/fv.kb.xB8 at switch values 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, and 17.
Kirby Sheik-copy air end uses specialn_sk_freefall_toggle to choose normal fall versus ftCo_80096900 freefall handling.
In ftPp_SpecialS.c, decompiled ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys, ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, and ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Enter.
Popo air up-B entry divides self_vel.x/y by Ice Climber attrs x84/x88, calls ftPp_SpecialHi_801218F8, consumes jumps, clears cmd_vars, and initializes fv.pp fields.

Popo air up-B start physics calls ftCommon_Fall with attrs x8C/x90, applies ftCommon_8007CEF4, then queries Nana via Player_GetEntityAtIndex and reads Nana's FtPart_L4thNb joint position when Nana is in SpecialHi_0..SpecialHi_5.

Popo throw animation checks cmd_vars[1], calls Nana readiness helper ftNn_Init_8012309C, may transition through ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, otherwise increments mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0 and calls ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0.
ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C handles ground versus air cleanup, calls ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68, changes to motion state 0x162, and sets x21F8 to ftCommon_8007F76C.
ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Enter resets throw/cmd/speciallw state, changes to ftPp_MS_SpecialAirLw, starts animation scripts, and assigns accessory4_cb to fn_80122D2C.
The decomp.dev bot reported 17 new 100% matches totaling +2676 bytes and the PR body reported main.dol SHA1 verification OK.
smart_moves: Factored repeated Kirby Ness-copy attribute reset into ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr, making the two end-animation matches share the same dat_attr copying pattern without inventing broader semantics.
Kept clone-character distinctions explicit with fv.kb.hat.kind switches: Pichu versus Pikachu, Giga Bowser versus Bowser, and Link versus Young Link all select separate ftKb_MS_* ranges.

Preserved exact ground-to-air transition behavior by checking ft_80082708(gobj) == GA_Ground, calling ftCommon_8007D5D4, carrying fp->cur_anim_frame through Fighter_ChangeMotionState, and using the original numeric flags.
Used targeted header prototypes only where the decompiled code needed typed callback or helper references, especially fn_80100E0C, fn_80105978, ftNn_Init_8012309C, and ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C.
Refined ftIceClimberAttributes only where new code proved float usage at x88, x8C, and x90, avoiding over-naming beyond available evidence.

Matched partner-dependent Ice Climbers behavior by retrieving Nana with Player_GetEntityAtIndex(player_id, 1), checking her motion range, and reading FtPart_L4thNb joint position rather than approximating with Popo-only state.
Used stack padding and local dummy arrays where needed, indicating attention to compiler stack layout in small fighter callbacks.
decomp_lessons: Kirby copy-special decomp often needs hat.kind-based motion-state selection; copied specials are not always a single motion ID path.
When decompiling collision callbacks that cross from ground to air, preserve current animation frame, transition flags, and callback reinstallation; these are behavior and matching sensitive.
Dat-attribute struct padding can often be split confidently when new code accesses adjacent floats; this PR exposed Popo x88/x8C/x90 through velocity scaling and fall parameters.
Callback assignment sites are useful evidence for adding prototypes even when some target functions remain address-style or still undecompiled.
Air-end special animations commonly branch on a dat_attr freefall toggle: zero enters ordinary fall, nonzero calls ftCo_80096900 with the toggle value.
For Ice Climbers, many Popo helpers depend on Nana's entity, motion ID range, or readiness helper; future decomp should preserve those partner checks before simplifying control flow.
PAD_STACK, u8 dummy arrays, nested scopes, repeated GET_FIGHTER calls, and temporary locals such as f32 anim_frame may be necessary to preserve the original compiler's register and stack behavior.
naming_conventions: Fighter special callbacks keep suffixes such as _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, _Coll, and _Enter.

Kirby copy-special files use ftKb_SpecialN* file/function prefixes for copied neutral-B families, with short source-character tags visible in motion names such as Ns, Sk, Pk, Pc, Kp, Gk, Lk, Cl, and Pr.
Kirby copied motion IDs follow ftKb_MS_<copy-prefix>Special[Air]N(Start/Loop/End) and sometimes R/L suffixes for direction-specific states.
Character kind enums use FTKIND_* names, e.g. FTKIND_PICHU, FTKIND_PIKACHU, FTKIND_GKOOPS, FTKIND_KOOPA, FTKIND_LINK, and FTKIND_CLINK.
Unidentified functions remain address-based, e.g. fn_80100E0C, fn_80105978, ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
Known Kirby dat_attr fields received semantic names such as specialn_ns_frames_to_loop_charge_ground and specialn_sk_freefall_toggle, while uncertain Popo fields stayed positional x88/x8C/x90.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(40), PAD_STACK(4 * 8), and local u8 padding arrays to match stack layout.
Introduced nested blocks and repeated GET_FIGHTER/gobj->user_data loads to control variable lifetimes and register allocation.
Stored fp->cur_anim_frame in a local f32 anim_frame before switch-based motion selection in collision transitions.
Preserved magic Fighter_ChangeMotionState flags including 0x0C4C5080, 0x0C4C5082, 0x0C4C5880, and 0x0C4C5092U.
Used chained cmd_vars resets matching the original pattern, e.g. fp->cmd_vars[0] = fp->cmd_vars[1] = fp->cmd_vars[2] = fp->cmd_vars[3] = 0.
Kept float comparison/order details such as 1.0f == fp->mv.pr.specialn.x34.x and explicit 1.0F/0.0F constants.
Returned Link-copy special indices with motion_id minus the relevant base start motion ID, avoiding a table or manually numbered cases.
Preserved callback pointer assignments after state transitions, including death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, x21F8, and accessory4_cb.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code 57.65%, +0.07%, +2676 bytes, with 17 new 100% matches.
The PR body stated all functions matched 100% via objdiff and main.dol was SHA1 verified OK.
follow_up_queries: Search remaining /// # markers in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c around ftKb_NsSpecialAirNStart_Anim, ftKb_NsSpecialAirNHold_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C, ftKb_PrSpecialNLoop_Coll, ftKb_PrSpecialNHit_Coll, and fn_80105978.
Look for other Kirby copy neutral-B collision callbacks that can reuse the hat.kind motion-selection and ground-to-air transition pattern from ftKb_SpecialNPk.c.
Resolve semantic names for ftIceClimberAttributes x88, x8C, and x90 after more Popo/Nana up-B physics code is decompiled.
Investigate ftNn_Init_8012309C behavior to better name the Nana readiness check used by ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim.
Audit callback targets fn_80100E0C and fn_80105978 once their bodies are decompiled so the callback roles in ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Coll can be named semantically.
Search ftPp_SpecialS.c remaining Ice Climbers up-B/down-B stubs such as ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim for similar partner and stack-padding patterns.
